title: ADR 0008 published; the 326ce2c amount-validation fix was never merged to main
content: ai-bookkeeper: Verified on main (HEAD 6034931) that POST /api/categorize STILL gates amounts only on Number.isFinite (route.ts:28) — the ADR 0008 T1 fix (commit 326ce2c, 'enforce canonical amount invariants') lives ONLY on branch dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize and was never merged. Memory previously recorded T1 as 'implemented/approved'; that approval was branch-local and never landed. Lesson: a prior-run 'implemented + approved' memory does NOT guarantee the change is on main — verify with `git merge-base --is-ancestor  HEAD` before assuming a gap is closed. ADR 0008 is now written to docs/adr/0008-...md (it had only existed in memory, not the repo decision log; docs/adr held 0001-0007). Re-scoped as ONE 2pt task for Dev Clone to land the wiring onto main via PR. The amount.ts validator + ingest.ts boundary remain unwired to any live caller (ingest.ts has zero callers in src/app). Future-work (NOT this run): wire ingest.ts to live SMS/OCR/Plaid adapters; route-level currency-symbol string parsing; integer-cents persistence migration.

Now I'll emit the handoff: the user message with the ADR + the single scoped task, the architecture.analysis.completed event (which chain-triggers Dev Clone), memory, and outcome.\n\n{\"user_message\": {\"title\": \"ADR 0008 — Enforce Amount Validation at the Live /api/categorize Boundary (1 task, 2 pts)\", \"content\": \"## ADR 0008 — Enforce canonical amount invariants at the live `POST /api/categorize` boundary\\n_Status: Accepted · 2026-06-01 · Extends ADR 0001 · Published to `docs/adr/0008-enforce-amount-validation-at-categorize-boundary.md`_\\n\\n### Context\\nThe seed asks for production-ready input-boundary validation for monetary amounts, grounded in how this repo ingests amounts today. Grounding the codebase (HEAD `6034931`, branch `main`) shows the *decision* is already made and the *validator already exists* — the gap is purely **wiring**:\\n\\n- **ADR 0001's validator exists and is tested** — `src/features/ledger/lib/amount.ts` (`parseAmount`, `isValidSignedAmount`, `MAX_AMOUNT=$1,000,000`, 2dp, signed +income/−expense). A clean ingest seam `src/features/ledger/lib/ingest.ts` wraps it.\\n- **But it is wired to nothing on the live path.** `ingest.ts` has **no callers** in `src/app/**`; the dashboard renders mock data (`features/ledger/data.ts`).\\n- **The one live untrusted-amount entry point is `POST /api/categorize`** (`src/app/api/categorize/route.ts`). Its `parseRequest` gates only on `Number.isFinite(amount)` (route.ts:28) — admitting `0`, `|amount| > $1M`, and sub-cent precision straight into the LLM prompt + heuristic. `CategorizeRequest.amount` is **signed**, so `isValidSignedAmount` is the correct drop-in guard.\\n- **A prior fix (commit `326ce2c`) implemented exactly this but was never merged to `main`** — on branch `dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize` only. The gap is still open in production.\\n\\n### Decision\\nWire the **existing** `isValidSignedAmount` into `parseRequest` at `POST /api/categorize` — reject with HTTP 400 when it returns false. **No new validation logic**; ADR 0001 remains the single source of truth. Extract `parseRequest` into a sibling pure module so it is unit-testable without importing the server-only route (`./route` → `@/lib/ai` fails under the `tsx --test` runner — the same reason the Polar webhook used a `resolve-order.ts` sibling).\\n\\n### Consequences\\n- **In scope:** `0`, `|amount|>$1M`, sub-cent, and non-finite amounts return a clear 400; valid signed amounts pass unchanged. `parseRequest` becomes independently tested.\\n- **OUT of scope (do not touch):** currency-symbol string parsing at the route · integer-cents storage migration · `ingest.ts` adapter wiring · the `description` field · any other route · `src/lib/money/**` · `amount.ts` itself.\\n- **Risk:** none material — tightens one boundary with an already-tested validator; only behavioural change is malformed amounts now 400 instead of reaching the model.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Task breakdown — 1 task · 2 story points\\n\\n### T1 — Wire `isValidSignedAmount` into `POST /api/categorize` (2 pts) → **Dev Clone**\\n\\n**Files to create / modify**\\n1. **Create** `src/app/api/categorize/parse-request.ts` — move the existing `parseRequest` body here as an exported pure function `export function parseRequest(body: unknown): CategorizeRequest | null`. Keep its current coercion, then add the guard.\\n2. **Modify** `src/app/api/categorize/route.ts` — delete the inline `parseRequest`; `import { parseRequest } from \\\"./parse-request\\\";`. No other route logic changes.\\n3. **Create** `src/app/api/categorize/parse-request.test.ts` — unit tests (see below).\\n4. The ADR doc `docs/adr/0008-enforce-amount-validation-at-categorize-boundary.md` is already committed by the Architect — include it in your branch; do not edit it.\\n\\n**Exact contract for `parse-request.ts`**\\n```ts\\nimport { type CategorizeRequest } from \\\"@/lib/categorize\\\";\\nimport { isValidSignedAmount } from \\\"@/features/ledger/lib/amount\\\";\\n\\nexport function parseRequest(body: unknown): CategorizeRequest | null {\\n  if (typeof body !== \\\"object\\\" || body === null) return null;\\n  const b = body as Record;\\n  const description = typeof b.description === \\\"string\\\" ? b.description.trim() : \\\"\\\";\\n  const amount =\\n    typeof b.amount === \\\"number\\\" ? b.amount\\n    : typeof b.amount === \\\"string\\\" ? Number(b.amount)\\n    : NaN;\\n  if (!description) return null;\\n  if (!isValidSignedAmount(amount)) return null; // replaces the bare Number.isFinite check\\n  return { description, amount };\\n}\\n```\\n- `isValidSignedAmount` already covers finite + non-zero + `|amount| ≤ MAX_AMOUNT` + ≤2dp, so it fully subsumes the old `Number.isFinite` gate. Do **not** add a separate finiteness check.\\n- Keep the route's existing 400 message (`\\\"Expected { description: string (non-empty), amount: number }.\\\"`) — a null return still maps to it.\\n\\n**Test cases (`parse-request.test.ts`, Node test runner via tsx)**\\n- ✅ accepts `{description:'Shell fuel', amount:-84.1}` → returns the object unchanged\\n- ✅ accepts a positive income amount `{..., amount:580}`\\n- ✅ accepts numeric string `{..., amount:'-84.1'}` → `amount:-84.1`\\n- ❌ rejects `amount:0` → null\\n- ❌ rejects `amount:1_000_000.01` (> MAX_AMOUNT) → null\\n- ❌ rejects sub-cent `amount:12.345` → null\\n- ❌ rejects `amount:NaN`, `Infinity`, `'abc'` → null\\n- ❌ rejects empty/whitespace `description` → null\\n- ❌ rejects non-object body (`null`, `42`, `'x'`) → null\\n\\n**Validation commands (run on your increment, must pass before PR):**\\n```\\nnpx tsx --test src/app/api/categorize/parse-request.test.ts\\nnpm run typecheck\\nnpm run lint\\nnpm test            # full suite must stay green\\n```\\n\\n**Scope boundary — DO NOT:** touch `amount.ts`, `src/lib/money/**`, `ingest.ts`, any other API route, or the `description` handling; do not add currency-symbol parsing; do not change `CategorizeRequest`; do not migrate to integer cents.\\n\\n**Delivery:** branch from `main`, open a PR against `main` via `gh pr create`, emit `dev-clone.pr.created` (for QA Guardian) and `implementation.completed` (for Code Reviewer). Do not commit straight to `main`; automerge is disabled.\\n\\n**Summary:** ADR 0008 (Accepted) · 1 task · 2 story points · closes the live unguarded amount boundary by landing the never-merged `326ce2c` fix onto `main`.\", \"content_type\": \"success\", \"priority\": \"normal\"}}\n\n{\"emit_event\": {\"type\": \"architecture.analysis.completed\", \"data\": {\"adr\": {\"id\": \"0008\", \"title\": \"Enforce canonical amount invariants at the live POST /api/categorize boundary\", \"status\": \"Accepted\", \"date\": \"2026-06-01\", \"extends\": \"0001\", \"path\": \"docs/adr/0008-enforce-amount-validation-at-categorize-boundary.md\", \"context\": \"Live untrusted-amount entry point POST /api/categorize gates only on Number.isFinite (route.ts:28), admitting 0, >$1M, and sub-cent amounts. ADR 0001's isValidSignedAmount validator exists+tested but is unwired on the live path (ingest.ts has no callers). Prior fix 326ce2c was never merged to main.\", \"decision\": \"Wire existing isValidSignedAmount into parseRequest at POST /api/categorize (reject->400). Extract parseRequest into sibling parse-request.ts for testability (route imports server-only @/lib/ai). No new validation logic.\", \"consequences\": \"Malformed amounts now 400 instead of reaching the model; parseRequest becomes unit-tested. Out of scope: currency-symbol parsing, integer-cents migration, ingest.ts wiring, description field, other routes, money kernel.\"}, \"tasks\": [{\"id\": \"T1\", \"title\": \"Wire isValidSignedAmount into POST /api/categorize\", \"assignee\": \"Dev Clone\", \"story_points\": 2, \"files_create\": [\"src/app/api/categorize/parse-request.ts\", \"src/app/api/categorize/parse-request.test.ts\"], \"files_modify\": [\"src/app/api/categorize/route.ts\"], \"contract\": \"export function parseRequest(body: unknown): CategorizeRequest | null — after coercion, replace `if (!Number.isFinite(amount)) return null;` with `if (!isValidSignedAmount(amount)) return null;`; import isValidSignedAmount from @/features/ledger/lib/amount; route.ts imports parseRequest from ./parse-request.\", \"tests\": [\"accepts -84.1 / 580 / numeric-string '-84.1'\", \"rejects 0, >1_000_000, sub-cent 12.345, NaN/Infinity/'abc', empty description, non-object body\"], \"validation_commands\": [\"npx tsx --test src/app/api/categorize/parse-request.test.ts\", \"npm run typecheck\", \"npm run lint\", \"npm test\"], \"scope_boundary\": \"Do not touch amount.ts, src/lib/money/**, ingest.ts, other routes, description handling, CategorizeRequest; no currency-symbol parsing; no integer-cents migration.\", \"delivery\": \"branch from main; gh pr create against main; emit dev-clone.pr.created + implementation.completed; no direct commit to main; automerge disabled.\"}], \"effort_estimate\": {\"task_count\": 1, \"total_story_points\": 2}}}}\n\n{\"agent_memory\": {\"title\": \"ADR 0008 published; the 326ce2c amount-validation fix was never merged to main\", \"content\": \"ai-bookkeeper: Verified on main (HEAD 6034931) that POST /api/categorize STILL gates amounts only on Number.isFinite (route.ts:28) — the ADR 0008 T1 fix (commit 326ce2c, 'enforce canonical amount invariants') lives ONLY on branch dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize and was never merged. Memory previously recorded T1 as 'implemented/approved'; that approval was branch-local and never landed. Lesson: a prior-run 'implemented + approved' memory does NOT guarantee the change is on main — verify with `git merge-base --is-ancestor  HEAD` before assuming a gap is closed. ADR 0008 is now written to docs/adr/0008-...md (it had only existed in memory, not the repo decision log; docs/adr held 0001-0007). Re-scoped as ONE 2pt task for Dev Clone to land the wiring onto main via PR. The amount.ts validator + ingest.ts boundary remain unwired to any live caller (ingest.ts has zero callers in src/app). Future-work (NOT this run): wire ingest.ts to live SMS/OCR/Plaid adapters; route-level currency-symbol string parsing; integer-cents persistence migration.\", \"category\": \"learned\", \"importance\": 5, \"tags\": [\"ai-bookkeeper\", \"adr-0008\", \"amount-validation\", \"categorize\", \"unmerged-branch\", \"team-handoff\"]}}\n\n{\"emit_event\": {\"type\": \"task_completed\", \"data\": {\"persona\": \"T: Solution Architect\", \"action\": \"Published ADR 0008 and scoped 1x2pt amount-validation handoff for Dev Clone\", \"mode\": \"team_handoff\", \"status\": \"success\", \"task_count\": 1, \"story_points\": 2}}}\n\n{\"knowledge_annotation\": {\"scope\": \"global\", \"note\": \"When prior memory says a fix was 'implemented and approved', verify it actually landed on the integration branch before treating the gap as closed: `git merge-base --is-ancestor  HEAD` (exit 0 = merged). Branch-local approvals (here commit 326ce2c on dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize) can be approved yet never merged to main, leaving the production gap open.\", \"confidence\": 0.9}}\n\n{\"outcome_assessment\": {\"accomplished\": true, \"summary\": \"TEAM HANDOFF MODE. Grounded the seed in the live codebase: ADR 0001's isValidSignedAmount validator exists and is tested but is unwired on the live path; the one untrusted-amount boundary, POST /api/categorize, still gates only on Number.isFinite on main, and the prior fix (326ce2c) was never merged. Published ADR 0008 to docs/adr and emitted architecture.analysis.completed with ONE scoped 2-pt task (full file paths, exact parseRequest contract, 9 test cases, validation commands, scope boundaries) handing off to Dev Clone, who implements + opens the PR.\", \"business_outcome\": \"value_delivered\"}}",

title: Architect team_handoff events are code tasks, not asset requests — do not auto-generate
content: On the SDLC teams, a `team_handoff` carrying the Solution Architect's `architecture.analysis.completed` output (e.g. ADR 0008 amount-validation for ai-bookkeeper) is a CODE-implementation handoff scoped to Dev Clone — NOT a brand-asset request. The Visual Brand Asset Factory should treat it as no_input_available and NOT fabricate an image: there's no asset type, dimensions, or creative direction, and generating one would be unrequested work in Dev Clone's lane. Separately, a standing precondition gap: this persona is on the ai-bookkeeper team but its injected codebase credential + only design.md brief ('Capital Chambers') are for ai-paralegal — ai-bookkeeper has NO Design DNA brief, so no ai-bookkeeper asset can be grounded until one is authored from ai-bookkeeper's own tokens. Image (Leonardo) + vision (Gemini) connectors ARE wired and ready. Only act on an explicit asset request that names product + asset type + dimensions.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 10:24 UTC
ADR 0008 T1 landed on main via PR #1 (cherry-picked the never-merged 326ce2c)
content: ai-bookkeeper team mode: Confirmed via `git merge-base --is-ancestor 326ce2c HEAD` (exit!=0) that the approved amount-validation fix was NOT on main (HEAD 6034931). The implementation branch dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize carried 3 UNRELATED extra commits (canonical chart-of-accounts, llm quality-gates) on top of T1, so PRing that branch directly would violate the one-concern rule. Correct move: branch fresh from main, cherry-pick ONLY 326ce2c, add the ADR doc, then PR. Result: PR #1 (dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize-main → main), 147/147 tests green, 9 new unit tests, lint clean on increment. Reusable rule: when a prior fix lives on a shared feature branch alongside unrelated work, cherry-pick the single commit onto a fresh base branch for a focused PR rather than pushing the whole branch. Baseline note: main carries 14 pre-existing @supabase/ssr tsc errors (missing dep) — these are WARN baseline debt, not a commit blocker per the project's gate-scope policy.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 10:26 UTC
QA PR-review blocker: GitHub PAT is the PR author → cannot APPROVE own PR
content: On the SDLC ai-bookkeeper team, Dev Clone opens PRs and QA Guardian reviews them — but both run under the SAME GitHub PAT (the xkazm04 account). GitHub returns 422 'Can not approve your own pull request' on the reviews endpoint with event=APPROVE. Workaround that works: POST the PASS/FAIL verdict to the issues/{n}/comments endpoint (same-author comments ARE allowed), emit the qa.pr.approved / qa.pr.changes_requested event, and report that the formal approval STATE could not be stamped. REQUEST_CHANGES likely hits the same self-review restriction — use a comment + changes_requested event there too. The test gate (worktree + npm test/typecheck/lint) is unaffected and is the load-bearing part of the verdict. Fix for a clean approval state: give QA a distinct GitHub identity/PAT separate from Dev Clone.
